key_insight: The paper shows that a 2D steady-state induction heating model can be made mathematically well-posed and accurately approximated with finite elements, even when the heat source is only weakly integrable.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a reliable FEM basis for predicting induction heating behavior in coupled electromagnetic-thermal simulations, with improved robustness from a bound-preserving heat solver.
method: Derive a natural weak formulation using a priori regularity results for the coupled PDEs.
Prove convergence of the standard Galerkin FEM under convex-domain and mesh-quality assumptions.
Apply a bound-preserving method (BPM) to the heat equation to obtain convergence under weaker domain/mesh conditions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports several numerical experiments that confirm the theory and demonstrate better performance for the bound-preserving method than standard FEM.
watch_item: The strongest convergence guarantees still depend on domain shape and mesh conditions, so practical implementations must control mesh quality and boundary geometry carefully.
paper_title: Analysis and approximation of a two-dimensional induction heating problem
